course_title: Professional Practice and the Business Environment B

learning_outcomes.description: Develop a business case for a project (start-up), incorporating key aspects that influence critical decision-gates
Explain and critique your roles and responsibilities as a professional engineer working in projects
Perform a financial analysis of a project proposal
Identify, assess and treat strategic business risks
Critique the role of sustainability in business decision-making
Work as part of a multi-disciplinary team of engineers to create project proposals and evaluate project feasibility

assessments.assessment_title: Team Project (Parts A, B, C, D, E)
assessments.due_date:
Part A Start-up Value Proposition (6%) 17/08/2026 12:00 pm

Part B Financial Analysis & BMC (6%) 7/09/2026 12:00 pm

Part C SSR Analysis (6%) 6/10/2026 1:00 pm

Part D Start-up Pitch Report (30%) 26/10/2026 12:00 pm

Part E Start-up Pitch presentation (12%) 26/10/2026

Slide sets for each team are due on Mondays 12:00pm (with exception of week 10, where Tuesday is the due date). In class interviews will be held during Week 4, 7, 10 Applied Class sessions. Pitch presentations will take place during the Week 13 Applied class sessions.
assessments.task_description:
Part A Start-up Value Proposition

Your task is to develop a start-up pitch. You are restricted to a capital ask of no more than AUD $1m. Your start-up can be contained fully within Australia or can target other countries or regions, as long as the location aligns with the start-up idea.  Your idea will ultimately be presented as a Pitch Presentation in Week 13 which will be targeted at your potential seed (angel) investors. Each team must submit a team charter and value proposition slide deck. Team Interviews on the Value Proposition will occur during the Applied Classes during Week 4. Supporting powerpoint should be submitted by 12:00 pm on the Monday of Week 4.

Part B Financial Analysis & Business Model Canvas

Considering a time line of 5 years, starting with 1 July 2026 (FY 27) as your timeline zero point up to FY31, and using an Excel™ DCF spreadsheet, construct a financial model for your start-up venture. Team Interviews on the Business Model Canvas will occur during the Applied Classes during Week 7. Supporting powerpoint should be submitted by 12:00 pm on the Monday of Week 7.

Part C Stakeholder Sustainability and Risk Analysis

Your task is to prepare a Stakeholder, Sustainability, and Risk Analysis evaluation for your start-up venture. This will be used to decide the next detailed development steps and where further study is needed. Team interviews on stakeholder, sustainability and risk will occur during the Applied Classes during Week 10. Supporting powerpoint should be submitted by 1:00 pm on Tuesday of Week 10.

Part D

Pitch report. A complete report covering all 3 sections of your project as a detailed report to your project manager. Due 12:00 pm on the Monday of Week 13. A PAF, limited in value between zero and a maximum of 1.1, will be applied.

Part E

The pitch summarises the business case around your start-up (Value Proposition, DCF Analysis and Stakeholder/Sustainability /Risk Analysis). A short proposal will be made to Angel Investors Pty Ltd, a venture capital company. The aim of the presentation is to convince an investor that your idea is worth investing in. In Class Pitch Presentation: Slides due by 12:00 pm on the Monday of Week 13. Presentations will occur during the Applied Classes during Week 13.


Please refer to Blackboard for a detailed marking criteria.

If, for whatever reason, you find that your group is not functioning effectively, please contact your Course Coordinator for support.

This task has been designed to be challenging, authentic and complex. Whilst students may use AI and/or MT technologies, successful completion of assessment in this course will require students to critically engage in specific contexts and tasks for which artificial intelligence will provide only limited support and guidance.

A failure to reference generative AI or MT use may constitute student misconduct under the Student Code of Conduct.

To pass this assessment, students will be required to demonstrate detailed comprehension of their written submission independent of AI and MT tools.
assessments.submission_guidelines: Submission is via turn-it-in on Blackboard Ultra.
assessments.deferral_or_extension: You may be able to apply for an extension.
assessments.late_submission: A penalty of 10% of the maximum possible mark will be deducted per 24 hours from time submission is due for up to 7 days. After 7 days, you will receive a mark of 0.
assessments.additional_info: You may be able to apply for an extension.

assessments.task_description:
An individual assignment. 

You are tasked with reflecting on the meaning of professional engineering practice. What is a professional engineer? When will you be a professional engineer? How does it affect how/what you do?

This assessment task evaluates students' abilities, skills and knowledge without the aid of generative Artificial Intelligence (AI) or Machine Translation (MT). Students are advised that the use of AI or MT technologies to develop responses is strictly prohibited and may constitute student misconduct under the Student Code of Conduct.
assessments.submission_guidelines: Submission is via turn-it-in on Blackboard Ultra.
assessments.deferral_or_extension: You may be able to apply for an extension.
assessments.late_submission: You will receive a mark of 0 if this assessment is submitted late.
assessments.additional_info: You may be able to apply for an extension.
